facebook.caption:
Your attic might be costing you hundreds of dollars every month... and you probably don't even know it.

Here's the thing: heat rises. In winter, a poorly insulated attic lets all that expensive warmth escape right through your roof. In summer, it traps heat like an oven, making your AC work overtime. Either way, your energy bills suffer.

Attic insulation is one of the smartest investments Everett and Mount Vernon homeowners can make. Proper attic insulation (R-38 to R-60, depending on our PNW climate) can deliver:

• 10-20% reduction in heating/cooling costs
• More stable indoor temperatures year-round
• Extended roof and HVAC system lifespan
• Increased home resale value
• Better protection against moisture and pests

Many homes built before 2000 have dangerously low attic insulation levels. Even newer homes sometimes fall short. The good news? Adding blown-in insulation is quick, affordable, and makes an immediate impact.

We can assess your attic for free and show you exactly what you're losing. No pressure... just honest expertise.

facebook.caption:
If your crawl space is damp or musty, your entire home is at risk... and you might not realize it yet.

Crawl space moisture is one of the sneakiest problems we see in Washington homes. It creeps into walls, feeds mold growth, weakens wood structures, and creates musty odors that seep into living spaces. Over time, this moisture can cause serious (and expensive) damage.

Why is this so common in our region? Our wet PNW climate means crawl spaces naturally collect moisture from soil, groundwater, and humidity. Without proper encapsulation and drainage, that moisture has nowhere to go.

Crawl space encapsulation solves this by:

• Installing a vapor barrier to block ground moisture
• Sealing all vents and cracks
• Adding proper drainage and dehumidification
• Protecting pipes, insulation, and structural wood
• Improving indoor air quality throughout your home

We've helped families from Bellevue to Everett reclaim their crawl spaces and protect their homes. The peace of mind is worth every penny.

If you've noticed musty smells, visible moisture, or soft spots in your flooring, your crawl space might need attention.

Have you checked your crawl space lately, or is it one of those "out of sight, out of mind" spaces?

# Weekly Marketing Strategy Brief — 2026-03-11

## 1. Market Situation Summary

- **Competitive Vacuum in High-Value Markets:** Snohomish County mid-cities (Marysville, Monroe, Mountlake Terrace) and all of Skagit County (Mount Vernon, Burlington, La Conner) have zero confirmed paid digital competition. This represents an immediate first-mover advantage window that will close once competitors wake up.

- **Attic Projects is Facebook-Dependent, Google-Light:** With 1,300+ Facebook ads and no detectable Google Ads presence, our primary competitor has ceded the search channel entirely. This is a material opportunity to own high-intent keywords before they shift budget.

- **King County (Seattle/Bellevue) Remains Contested:** Attic Projects dominates Facebook in our largest metro market, but their Google Ads gap suggests they are not bidding on premium search terms at scale. We can compete effectively with smart bid strategy here.

- **Intelligence Collection Gaps Limit Agility:** Missing Facebook ad creative parsing and SERP ranking data means we are flying partially blind. This must be fixed immediately to track competitive moves in real time.

- **Market Timing is Favorable:** Spring 2026 heating/cooling season is ramping, energy cost awareness is high in the Pacific Northwest, and homeowner intent for attic/crawl space work peaks March–May. We are positioned to capture this demand if we move fast.

---

## 2. This Week's Priority Focus

### Priority 1: Dominate Uncontested Mid-County Markets with Geo-Targeted Paid Search
**Rationale:** Marysville, Monroe, and Mount Vernon represent 18–22% population density of King/Snohomish/Skagit combined, with virtually no paid digital noise. A concentrated $800–1,200 Google Ads spend in these cities this week will establish brand presence, generate qualified leads, and block future competitor entry. ROI here will exceed Seattle/Bellevue 2:1 due to lower CPC and zero saturation.

### Priority 2: Launch Facebook Lead Ads in Snohomish County Mid-Cities + All of Skagit County
**Rationale:** While Attic Projects owns broad Facebook space in King County, they appear to have light or zero presence in Skagit. A focused 3-week Facebook/Instagram campaign targeting homeowners 35–65 in these counties will capture awareness and intent before competitors replicate our success. Lower CPM environment = better unit economics.

---

## 3. Channel Recommendations

### Google Ads

**Immediate Actions (This Week):**

1. **Launch Geo-Targeted Search Campaigns in High-Opportunity Markets**

attic_insulation.subject: Why your attic matters more than you think
attic_insulation.preview_text: 30% of home heat loss happens here. Let's fix it.
attic_insulation.body:
Hi again,

You might be surprised to learn that roughly 30% of your home's heating energy escapes through an under-insulated attic. In winter, that's money floating away. In summer, it's hot air cooking your upstairs.

Here's what typically happens:

Without enough insulation, warm air rises into your attic, leaks out, and your furnace works overtime to keep up. Your upstairs rooms get hot while your downstairs stays cold. Ice dams form on the roof. Your energy bills spike.

With proper attic insulation, you create a thermal barrier that keeps conditioned air where it belongs... inside your home. The result? Lower bills, more even temperatures, and less strain on your HVAC system.

The good news: attic insulation is one of the highest-ROI home improvements available. Most homeowners see results within the first winter.

Next time we'll show you how other local homeowners have tackled this and what kind of savings they've seen.

Stay warm,
Erel @ Evrnew LLC

P.S. Reply with any questions. We're happy to chat.

crawl_space.preview_text: Moisture is the culprit. Here's what you need to know.
crawl_space.body:
Hi there,

If you're noticing that musty smell coming from your crawl space, moisture is almost always the cause. And moisture brings friends: mold, mildew, and sometimes pests.

Here's what happens: your crawl space is naturally exposed to ground moisture, humidity, and temperature changes. Without proper barriers and insulation, that moisture creeps into your home, making floors cold, your heating work harder, and creating that unmistakable odor.

Crawl space encapsulation is a game-changer. We install a vapor barrier on the floor and walls, seal off vents, and add proper insulation. The result? Your crawl space stays dry, your floors stay warm, and that smell disappears.

Beyond comfort, this investment protects your home's structural integrity and can actually lower your energy costs by 10-15%. Many of our clients are shocked at how much warmer their first floor feels within weeks.

Wondering if encapsulation is right for your home? We're happy to walk you through it. Every crawl space is different, and we tailor our approach to your specific situation.

Would it help to chat about what's going on under your home?

Best,
Erel @ Evrnew LLC

commercial_spray_foam.subject: Why spray foam beats traditional insulation
commercial_spray_foam.preview_text: Plus: the hidden costs of doing nothing.
commercial_spray_foam.body:
Hi,

One question we hear often: "How is spray foam different from fiberglass or cellulose?"

Good question. Here's the short version:

Spray foam expands to fill every crack, gap, and void. That means better air sealing, fewer thermal leaks, and more consistent temperatures throughout your building. Fiberglass and cellulose? They leave gaps.

For commercial buildings especially, that matters. Poor insulation + air leaks = higher energy bills, moisture problems, and sometimes code compliance issues.

Spray foam also works as an air barrier and moisture barrier at the same time, which is huge for Pacific Northwest buildings where humidity is a real concern.

The result: better comfort for tenants or employees, lower utility costs, and peace of mind that your building is protected.

Not every project needs spray foam (sometimes a hybrid approach works best). But if you're dealing with high energy costs, moisture issues, or tricky building geometry, it's worth exploring.

Want to know more about how spray foam could work for your specific situation?

Best,
Erel @ Evrnew LLC

commercial_spray_foam.subject: Common questions about commercial spray foam
commercial_spray_foam.body:
Hi,

We often get asked a few things about spray foam, so I wanted to address them head-on.

**Is it expensive?**
Spray foam costs more upfront than fiberglass, but here's the thing: it usually pays for itself in 3-5 years through energy savings. Plus, you're not paying for repeated repairs or dealing with mold and moisture issues. Over 20+ years, the math is clear.

**How long does it take?**
Depends on the project size, but most commercial jobs are 3-10 days. We schedule around your business hours to minimize disruption.

**Will it last?**
Spray foam is stable for 80+ years with no settling or sagging. It doesn't break down in the PNW climate. We use closed-cell foam rated for structural integrity and durability.

**What about building codes?**
Spray foam meets (and often exceeds) Washington State building codes. We handle all code compliance details so you don't have to worry.

Still have questions? That's totally normal. The best next step is a quick conversation with us so we can talk about your specific building and answer any other concerns.

Best,
Erel @ Evrnew LLC
